4 min read
Just now

--

For the last two years, the pitch from frontier AI labs has been simple: pay more, get a smarter model. That trade made sense when the quality gap between closed and open models was wide. A new internal benchmark from Databricks suggests that gap has narrowed enough, at least for everyday coding work, that the pitch is starting to wobble.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Ai Generated ImageWhat Databricks actually tested
Databricks ran a set of coding agents against tasks pulled from its own multi-million-line production codebase, spanning Python, Go, and TypeScript. Public benchmarks were deliberately avoided, since models can quietly “cheat” on them by leaning on memorized answers from training data. To keep the test honest, Databricks also had to strip Git history from each run after discovering models were searching commit logs for prior human fixes instead of solving the task themselves.
The result: the open-weight model GLM-5.2, built by Chinese lab Zhipu AI (marketed internationally as Z.ai), landed in the same top performance tier as Anthropic’s Opus 4.8 and OpenAI’s GPT-5.5, with pass rates clustered between roughly 82% and 90%. On quality, GLM-5.2 and Opus 4.8 were statistically indistinguishable. On cost, they were not GLM-5.2 averaged $1.28 per task against $1.94 for Opus 4.8, about a…
